cards.name: Thornback Herbalist
cards.oracle_text: When ~ enters, you may search your library for a basic land card, reveal it, put it into your hand, then shuffle.
cards.flavor_text: Every root knows the path to water, every leaf the way to light.
cards.design_notes: A simple green common that provides utility through land searching, which is core to green's color pie. The 1/2 body for 2 mana is slightly below rate, which is appropriate given the enters-the-battlefield value. This supports green's ramp strategy while being simple enough for common. The optional nature prevents it from being mandatory deck thinning in competitive formats.

cards.name: Temple Guardian
cards.flavor_text: "The faithful need not fear the darkness, for I stand watch through the longest nights."
cards.design_notes: Simple white common with vigilance. Stats are 2/1 for 2 mana, which is fair for a creature with an evergreen keyword. Vigilance fits white's theme of protection and defense, and the Cleric type supports tribal synergies.

cards.name: Ember Striker
cards.oracle_text:
Haste
When ~ enters, ~ deals 1 damage to any target.
cards.flavor_text: Strike first, ask questions while running away.
cards.design_notes: Red common with haste and a small damage trigger on entry. 2/2 for 3 mana with upside is appropriate for common. The damage effect is very red and provides immediate board impact. Goblin Warrior fits red's aggressive tribal themes.

cards.name: Tidepool Scholar
cards.flavor_text: Every ripple in the water tells a story to those who know how to listen.
cards.design_notes: Blue common with defensive stats and card selection. 1/3 for 2 mana with scry 1 provides value without being overpowered. Scry is a blue evergreen ability that fits the scholar theme. The defensive statline is appropriate for blue creatures at common.

cards.name: Steadfast Sentinel
cards.flavor_text: "My watch does not end with the setting sun."
cards.design_notes: Simple white common with vigilance. 2/1 for 2 mana with upside is appropriate for common white creatures. Vigilance fits white's defensive nature while allowing aggressive play.

cards.name: Tidecaller Apprentice
cards.flavor_text: The first lesson is learning to listen to the whispers of the waves.
cards.design_notes: Blue common with card selection. 2/2 for 3 with scry 1 is a fair rate for common. Scry fits blue's information advantage philosophy and provides minor value without being too complex.

cards.name: Crypt Lurker
cards.flavor_text: It remembers only hunger and the taste of fear.
cards.design_notes: Black common with deathtouch. 3/2 deathtouch for 4 mana is appropriately costed for common. The double black requirement makes it more committed to black strategies. Deathtouch is quintessentially black and creates interesting combat decisions.

cards.name: Emberhorn Raider
cards.flavor_text: The sound of thundering hooves is the last thing many enemies hear.
cards.design_notes: Red common with haste. 3/1 haste for 3 mana with double red requirement fits red's aggressive philosophy. Glass cannon design with high power but low toughness, typical of red creatures. Haste enables immediate pressure.

cards.name: Thornwood Guardian
cards.flavor_text: The ancient groves have their own ways of dealing with unwelcome visitors.
cards.design_notes: Green common with reach. 2/5 reach for 4 mana is a defensive creature that can block flyers, fitting green's anti-flying philosophy. High toughness over power reflects green's ability to create durable creatures. Good defensive option for green decks.

cards.name: Steadfast Squire
cards.flavor_text: "My watch begins at dawn and ends at dawn."
cards.design_notes: Simple white aggressive creature with vigilance. 2/1 for 2 mana is fair stats with the upside of attacking without tapping.

cards.name: Temple Guardian
cards.flavor_text: The sacred halls have never known violence while she stands watch.
cards.design_notes: Defensive white creature with lifegain ETB. 2/4 body is good for blocking, and the lifegain provides incremental value typical of white commons.

cards.name: Tide Pool Scholar
cards.flavor_text: Every ripple in the water tells a story to those who know how to read it.
cards.design_notes: Blue creature with card selection. 1/2 for 2 is below rate but scry 1 provides value. Fits blue's philosophy of information and preparation.

cards.name: Stormcrest Sailor
cards.flavor_text: "The higher the mast, the farther you can see trouble coming."
cards.design_notes: Vanilla flying creature for blue. 2/2 flying for 3 mana is the standard rate for common flyers. Clean and simple design.

cards.name: Crypt Lurker
cards.flavor_text: It remembers only hunger and the way flesh tears.
cards.design_notes: Aggressive 1-mana creature with deathtouch. 1/1 deathtouch for 1 mana is fair - trades with any creature but dies to most removal.

cards.name: Bone Collector
cards.oracle_text: When ~ enters, target opponent discards a card.
cards.flavor_text: "The dead have such interesting stories to tell."
cards.design_notes: Black creature with discard effect. 2/2 for 3 is below rate but the ETB provides card advantage, fitting black's disruptive nature.

cards.name: Emberkin Warrior
cards.flavor_text: Born from the mountain's fury, it knows only the need to charge.
cards.design_notes: Aggressive red 1-drop with haste. 2/1 haste for 1 mana is the classic red aggressive creature template - immediate pressure but fragile.

cards.name: Forge Apprentice
cards.oracle_text: When ~ enters, ~ deals 1 damage to any target.
cards.flavor_text: "Oops" is the most common word in any goblin workshop.
cards.design_notes: Red creature with direct damage ETB. 2/2 for 3 with 1 damage to any target provides immediate board impact, fitting red's aggressive and direct nature.

cards.name: Rootbound Tracker
cards.flavor_text: The canopy is her domain, and nothing passes through unnoticed.
cards.design_notes: Solid green creature with reach. 2/2 reach for 2 mana is slightly above rate, giving green good defensive utility against flyers while maintaining board presence.

cards.name: Ironwood Behemoth
cards.flavor_text: When ancient trees take root in its hide, it becomes a living forest.
cards.design_notes: Large green creature with trample. 4/3 trample for 4 mana represents green's philosophy of large creatures that can push damage through. Good rate with relevant keyword.
